summary: The Docker-based reproduction builds Traefik v1.7.34 from source with the REST provider enabled, then injects an arbitrary routing configuration via an unauthenticated PUT request to /api/providers/rest. The injected backend-evil and evil.example.com frontend appear in the active configuration, demonstrating full control over the routing plane.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction should allow an unauthenticated network client to inject arbitrary frontends and backends into the Traefik routing configuration via a PUT request, causing the injected rules to become active immediately.
actual_observed_result: The PUT /api/providers/rest request returned HTTP 200, and a subsequent GET /api/providers confirmed that the injected backend-evil and evil.example.com frontend route are present in the active configuration.
verification_method: The verify script sends the PUT request and then performs GET /api/providers to confirm the injected backend name (backend-evil) and frontend route (evil.example.com) appear in the active provider configuration. The grep-based check distinguishes successful injection from a generic startup failure or empty config.
evidence_produced_by_run: PUT /api/providers/rest returned HTTP 200, confirming the configuration was accepted.
GET /api/providers response contains the injected backend-evil backend definition.
GET /api/providers response contains the injected evil.example.com frontend route.
No authentication was required to inject the configuration.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable ref v1.7.34, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
